title: Monsoon Update – Thunderstorm and Rain Alert in 17 States till March 31, Know the Weather Forecast - Times Bull
article_text:
Monsoon Alert: The weather is set to take a turn once again, a shift that could lead to a rather alarming situation. Rain in some areas—and hailstorms accompanied by thunder in others—are expected to disrupt normal daily life. Severe disruptions are anticipated across 17 states, including Delhi-NCR, Uttar Pradesh, and Punjab. Wind speeds could also reach up to 80 kilometres per hour.
Consequently, there is a looming risk of trees and electricity poles toppling over. In the hilly regions, temperatures continue to be recorded at significantly low levels due to ongoing snowfall. According to the India Meteorological Department (IMD), warnings for heavy rainfall have been issued for several states across the country. You can find detailed information regarding the specific weather conditions expected in various locations below.
According to the Meteorological Department, there is a forecast for rain in several districts of Uttar Pradesh, including Kanpur, Lucknow, Auraiya, Kannauj, Hardoi, Shahjahanpur, and Mahamayangar. In addition to these, heavy rainfall is also possible in Lalitpur, Jhansi, Sitapur, Mainpuri, Aligarh, Sambhal, and Bijnor. Furthermore, Bahraich, Farrukhabad, Meerut, Jyotiba Phule Nagar, and Pilibhit may experience rain accompanied by storms and thunder.
According to the IMD, a warning for heavy rainfall has been issued for Patna, West Champaran, East Champaran, Siwan, and Bhojpur. There is a forecast for rain accompanied by storms in Saran, Gopalganj, Buxar, Gaya, Muzaffarpur, Vaishali, Samastipur, Darbhanga, Madhubani, Supaul, Saharsa, Purnia, and Araria.
According to the Meteorological Department, an alert has been issued for heavy rainfall accompanied by thunder and lightning in Dehradun, Nainital, Pithoragarh, Bageshwar, Chamoli, Tehri Garhwal, Almora, and Champawat. An alert has also been issued regarding wind speeds that could reach up to 60 kilometres per hour in these areas. **Rain Expected in Punjab as Well**
According to the IMD, there is a possibility of rain accompanied by thunderstorms and squalls in Pathankot, Amritsar, Ludhiana, Hoshiarpur, Kapurthala, Gurdaspur, Jalandhar, and Ferozepur. Given the likelihood of lightning strikes, an appeal has been issued to exercise caution.
According to the Meteorological Department, a moderate rainfall alert has been issued for Ambala, Sonipat, Gurugram, Kaithal, Panipat, Rewari, Hisar, Yamunanagar, and Mahendragarh. During this period, winds are expected to blow at speeds of up to 30 kilometres per hour.
According to the IMD, an alert for moderate to heavy rainfall has been issued for Shimla, Chamba, Kullu, Mandi, Lahaul-Spiti, Kangra, Hamirpur, and Manali. An alert for the occurrence of squalls has also been issued for these areas.
According to the IMD, there is a possibility of thunderstorms, lightning, and strong winds in Madhya Pradesh and Vidarbha from March 30 to April 2, and in Chhattisgarh over the next six days. Light to moderate rainfall is also anticipated. Rain accompanied by thunder may also occur in Himachal Pradesh, Rajasthan, and Jharkhand. Additionally, rain is expected in Jammu & Kashmir and Western Rajasthan on March 30, as well as in Uttarakhand.

title: Heavy Rainfall Alert for Northeast and Eastern India - udaipurkiran.com
article_text:
Heavy Rainfall Alert for Northeast and Eastern India
30 Mar 2026 9:48 AM by Bhupendra Singh Chundawat

New Delhi, March 30: The India Meteorological Department (IMD) has issued an orange alert for heavy rainfall in Northeast and Eastern India. The department forecasts continuous heavy rain in the northeastern states until tomorrow. Additionally, thunderstorms with rain are expected in the plains of West Bengal, Himachal Pradesh, and Odisha. There are also warnings of thunderstorms and lightning in Punjab, Bihar, Chhattisgarh, Jharkhand, Telangana, and western Rajasthan.

The IMD has specifically issued an orange alert for heavy rainfall in Arunachal Pradesh, Assam, Meghalaya, sub-Himalayan West Bengal, and Sikkim. Hailstorms are likely in Bihar, West Bengal, and Sikkim today.

Similar weather conditions are expected in Ladakh, Jammu and Kashmir, Andhra Pradesh, Kerala, Mahe, South Interior Karnataka, Vidarbha, and the northeastern states. Coastal Karnataka will experience hot and humid weather until tomorrow.

According to the weather department, between March 30 and 31, winds will blow at speeds of 30 to 50 kilometers per hour in Delhi, Uttar Pradesh, Punjab, Haryana, Chandigarh, and Rajasthan, accompanied by rain. Heavy rainfall with winds reaching 60 kilometers per hour is expected in Himachal Pradesh, Punjab, and eastern Rajasthan today. An alert has been issued for Uttarakhand due to the forecast of heavy winds and rain across large areas on March 30-31.

Moreover, hail is expected in some areas of Jammu and Kashmir and western Rajasthan today, as well as in Uttarakhand. The IMD predicts light to moderate rainfall with thunderstorms and lightning in Madhya Pradesh, Vidarbha, and parts of Chhattisgarh between March 30 and April 2.

Bhupendra Singh Chundawat

Bhupendra Singh Chundawat is a seasoned technology journalist with over 22 years of experience in the media industry. He specializes in covering the global technology landscape, with a deep focus on manufacturing trends and the geopolitical impact on tech companies. Currently serving as the Editor at Udaipur Kiran, his insights are shaped by decades of hands-on reporting and editorial leadership in the fast-evolving world of technology.
